summary: Tested 3 bug fixes for Actor's Companion Feature #5. Backend fixes #2 (filter SELF-TAPE INSTRUCTIONS, WARDROBE, PERFORMANCE, TAKES, READER, DEADLINE, etc.) VERIFIED PASSING via new pytest case. Frontend fix #3 (post-confirm routes to prep-placeholder with Change character + Back to projects buttons, Change character returns to character-select) VERIFIED PASSING via Playwright e2e. Frontend fix #1 (reopening project with selected_character should route to prep-placeholder, not character-select) FAILS — the fix was NOT applied in App.js. handleOpenProject (lines 32-53) only checks allConfirmed; it does not consult proj.selected_character and always routes to 'characters' when all docs are confirmed. Reproduced live: 'PROJECT NIGHT VISIT' with backend selected_character=OLIVER reopens to character-select instead of prep-placeholder.

ui_bugs.issue: Fix #1 NOT applied. After all docs confirmed, always routes to 'characters' view regardless of selected_character. Should route to 'prep' when proj.selected_character is truthy. Reproduced: opening PROJECT NIGHT VISIT (backend has selected_character=OLIVER) shows character-select instead of prep-placeholder, causing the routing loop the fix was supposed to eliminate.

action_items: Fix handleOpenProject in /app/frontend/src/App.js to check proj.selected_character when allConfirmed. Suggested change inside the else branch at line ~45: if (proj.selected_character) { setView('prep'); } else { setView('characters'); }

After fix, retest: (a) reopen PROJECT NIGHT VISIT -> should show prep-placeholder with 'Playing: OLIVER' (or current value); (b) reopen a project that has all docs confirmed but no selected_character -> should still show character-select.
critical_code_review_comments: handleOpenProject is missing the selected_character branch entirely — the PR description in the problem statement does not match the committed code. Either the change was lost in a later edit or never saved.
The prep-placeholder view correctly uses optional chaining (activeProject.selected_character?.[0]) which handles the null case gracefully — good defensive coding.
Backend filter list is well-scoped to EXACT matches (skip_exact set) so it won't accidentally filter multi-word character names like 'DETECTIVE JONES' or 'ALEX INSTRUCTOR'. Good implementation.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_LabelFilter, TEST_NoConfirm, TEST_MultiDoc, TEST_CharDetect via pytest/API. Test data removed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fix #1 is the only remaining bug. It requires a ~3 line change in App.js handleOpenProject to branch on proj.selected_character. Backend filter (fix #2) fully working — regression covered by test_detect_characters_filters_label_words. Post-confirm routing (fix #3) and prep-placeholder UI (change-character-btn, prep-back-home-btn) verified working. testids in use: prep-placeholder, change-character-btn, prep-back-home-btn, character-select, confirm-character-btn. Use PROJECT NIGHT VISIT project (id edcb57df-ae4d-4112-8cb6-418df93d5f0d) for reopen testing — note that its selected_character was overwritten to MAYA during this run because I clicked confirm with the auto-selected top character; set it back via PUT /api/projects/{id} {selected_character:'OLIVER'} before retesting if needed.
rca of the issue: RCA fix #1: handleOpenProject in /app/frontend/src/App.js currently implements: `if (!allConfirmed) setView('project'); else setView('characters');` — it never inspects proj.selected_character. Expected per problem statement: when all documents are confirmed AND proj.selected_character is set, setView('prep'); otherwise setView('characters'). Reproduction: GET /api/projects returns PROJECT NIGHT VISIT with selected_character='OLIVER' and all documents confirmed; clicking the project card on home triggers handleOpenProject which routes to 'characters' view (CharacterSelect) — confirmed visually. Mitigation: add `if (proj.selected_character) { setView('prep'); } else { setView('characters'); }` inside the allConfirmed branch.
